SEARCH
Package io.stimulustech.filequeue.store

Class MVStoreQueue

java.lang.Object
io.stimulustech.filequeue.store.MVStoreQueue
All Implemented Interfaces:
Comparable<MVStoreQueue>
public final class MVStoreQueue extends Object implements Comparable<MVStoreQueue>
Fast queue implementation on top of MVStore. This class is thread-safe.
Author:
Valentin Popov, Jamie Band

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    MVStoreQueue(Path queueDir, String queueName)
    This class is for internal use only.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    void
    clear()
    Removes all items from the queue map.
    void
    close()
    Closes this filequeue and frees up all resources associated to it.

    Commits any pending changes to the underlying MVStore.

    Compares this queue to another by directory path then name, consistent with equals(java.lang.Object).

    Returns the directory where the queue database files are stored.

    Determines if this filequeue is empty (equivalent to size() == 0).

    Retrieves and and removes element from the head of this filequeue.

    Pushes element to the tail of this filequeue.

    Reopens the underlying MVStore, discarding any previous open handle.

    Returns the size of this filequeue.

  • Constructor Details

    • MVStoreQueue

      public MVStoreQueue(Path queueDir, String queueName) throws IOException
      This class is for internal use only. Please refer to FileQueue class.
      Parameters:
      queueDir - filequeue database environment directory path
      queueName - descriptive filequeue name
      Throws:
      IOException - thrown when the given queueEnvPath does not exist and cannot be created.

  • Method Details

    • reopen

      public void reopen() throws IllegalStateException
      Reopens the underlying MVStore, discarding any previous open handle. Safe to call after close() to re-use the same queue directory.
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if the store cannot be opened

    • getQueueDir

      public Path getQueueDir()
      Returns the directory where the queue database files are stored.
      Returns:
      absolute path of the queue database directory

    • poll

      public byte[] poll()
      Retrieves and and removes element from the head of this filequeue.
      Returns:
      element from the tail of the filequeue or null if filequeue is empty

    • push

      public void push(byte[] element)
      Pushes element to the tail of this filequeue.
      Parameters:
      element - byte array containing element data

    • clear

      public void clear()
      Removes all items from the queue map. The store is not compacted or committed; call commit() afterwards if persistence is required.

    • size

      public long size()
      Returns the size of this filequeue.
      Returns:
      the size of the filequeue

    • isEmpty

      public boolean isEmpty()
      Determines if this filequeue is empty (equivalent to size() == 0).
      Returns:
      true if this filequeue is empty, otherwise false.

    • close

      public void close()
      Closes this filequeue and frees up all resources associated to it.

    • compareTo

      public int compareTo(@Nonnull MVStoreQueue o)
      Compares this queue to another by directory path then name, consistent with equals(java.lang.Object). Two queues are ordered by their storage directory first; within the same directory they are ordered by queue name.
      Specified by:
      compareTo in interface Comparable<MVStoreQueue>

    • equals

      public boolean equals(Object o)
      Overrides:
      equals in class Object

    • hashCode

      public int hashCode()
      Overrides:
      hashCode in class Object

    • commit

      public void commit()
      Commits any pending changes to the underlying MVStore. This method is a no-op when the store has already been closed, making it safe to call from a finally block even after close() has returned.
